sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The board changed (board_changed: true), so the green center moved. Looking at the image, the green center appears to still be in the bottom container area. Let me check its exact position.
Actually, looking at the image more carefully, the green center is at rows 40-41, cols 12-13 (middle of the bottom container). It moved up one step from rows 42-43 to rows 40-41.
So the green center moved up within the container, not to the middle row. This is different from what I expected. Earlier, when the green center was at rows 42-43, moving up should have taken it to the middle row. But now it only moved up one step within the container.
This suggests that the green center's movement depends on the position of the colored blocks. When the blue block is at rows 34-37, cols 8-11 (blocking the path), the green center cannot move up to the middle row.
Let me check the current positions of all objects.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check green center and blue block positions
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['N', 'b']:
        b = n['boundary']
        r = (min(p[0] for p in b), max(p[0] for p in b))
        c = (min(p[1] for p in b), max(p[1] for p in b))
        print(f"{n['color']}: px={n['pixels']}, rows={r}, cols={c}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see the issue. The blue dots are now scattered as individual pixels at rows 20-23, cols 18-21 (8 separate 1-pixel objects). The blue block is at rows 34-37, cols 8-11 (16 pixels). The green center is at rows 38-39, cols 12-13.
The blue dots being scattered at rows 20-23 might be blocking the green center's path to the middle row. Let me try clicking the blue target again to swap the blue block and blue dots back, which should consolidate the blue dots into a block and move the blue block to rows 20-23.
Actually wait - looking at the segmentation, the blue dots are 8 separate 1-pixel objects at rows 20-23, cols 18-21. This is the same pattern as the blue block's original position. So clicking the blue target swapped the blue block (16 px) with the blue dots (8 px), but the blue dots are now scattered.
Let me click the blue target again to swap them back.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Click blue target to swap blue block and blue dots back
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 36, 'col': 48}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
